text.title: java-junit-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for java-junit-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using @SpringBootTest instead of @WebMvcTest for controller tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: @SpringBootTest spins up the entire application context (database, all beans) on every test class. Tests become slow (multi-second startup). Use @WebMvcTest for controller-only tests.

failure-mode.description: Using H2 in-memory database for integration tests that depend on PostgreSQL-specific features (JSONB, window functions, sequences).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: H2 silently skips unsupported SQL, causing tests to pass that would fail in production. Use Testcontainers PostgreSQL for reality-based testing.

failure-mode.description: N+1 queries as the default outcome of FetchType.LAZY collections plus naive iteration.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lazy collections are not fetched until accessed. Iterating over a collection in the service layer fires one query per item. Use @EntityGraph or JOIN FETCH inside a @Transactional method to load the collection eagerly, within the transactional boundary.

failure-mode.description: @OneToMany(cascade = ALL, orphanRemoval = true) on relations where the child is referenced elsewhere.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Looks tidy but corrupts data. orphanRemoval=true deletes the child when it is removed from the parent collection, even if other entities still reference it. Always justify cascade and orphanRemoval in comments, and test the scenario where the child has multiple parents.

failure-mode.description: Confusing merge() vs save() vs persist() semantics; agents pick the wrong one.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: merge() re-attaches a detached entity and updates it; persist() inserts a new one; save() is Spring Data, not JPA. Test agents will produce duplicate rows or detached-entity exceptions. Code review must verify the right method is used for the domain operation.

failure-mode.description: LazyInitializationException outside transactions — accessing lazy collections in a controller method.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hibernate session is closed at the end of the service transaction. Accessing u.getRoles() in the controller triggers a query on a closed session. Use JOIN FETCH or @EntityGraph inside the service before the session closes.

failure-mode.description: Hibernate 6+ changed query parameter handling and implicit casts; copy-pasted code from old Stack Overflow answers compiles but throws at runtime.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Verify code against the active Hibernate major version. If migrating, test all JPQL queries in a fresh environment.

failure-mode.description: Schema drift between @Entity and Flyway/Liquibase migrations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Entity expects a column that does not exist in the database. spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=validate (the correct setting) throws InvalidDataAccessResourceUsageException on startup. CI passes (uses ddl-auto=create for tests) but production fails immediately. Pair every @Entity change with a migration in the same PR.

failure-mode.description: Using H2 in-memory database for integration tests that depend on PostgreSQL-specific features.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: H2 silently skips unsupported SQL and relational constraints. Tests pass that fail in production. Use Testcontainers PostgreSQL for reality-based testing.
